topStories.headline: Late Díaz and Olise fire Bayern past Real Madrid — Arsenal hold their nerve to reach the semis
body: It was one of the great Champions League nights at the Allianz Arena. Aleksandar Pavlovic put Bayern ahead in the 6th minute, Harry Kane made it two in the 38th, but Real Madrid roared back to make it 3–3 before Luis Díaz curled inside the right post in the 89th minute. Michael Olise sealed the deal in stoppage time — Bayern win 4–3 on the night, 6–4 on aggregate, and Vinícius Júnior's heroics weren't enough to save Madrid.

At the Emirates, Arsenal were nervous but disciplined, holding Sporting CP to a 0–0 draw to advance 1–0 on aggregate. The UCL semifinals are set: PSG vs. Bayern Munich (April 28 in Paris) and Atlético Madrid vs. Arsenal (April 29 in Madrid). The final is at Puskás Arena in Budapest on May 30.

topStories.headline: Maxey erupts and Curry leads a 43-point fourth quarter — 76ers and Warriors are in the playoffs
body: Tyrese Maxey dropped 31 points including 11 in a dominant fourth quarter as Philadelphia outlasted Orlando 109–97, earning the East 7-seed. The Sixers face the second-seeded Boston Celtics in Round 1. In the West, Stephen Curry hit the tie-breaking 3-pointer with 50 seconds left and finished with 35 points as Golden State rallied from 13 down to stun the Clippers 126–121. Al Horford hit four 3-pointers in a blistering fourth quarter where the Warriors outscored LA 43 to however many — a comeback for the ages.

Tonight, two elimination games decide the final playoff seeds. The Charlotte Hornets — who ousted Miami in overtime behind LaMelo Ball — travel to face the Orlando Magic at 7:30 ET (ESPN). In the West, the Warriors head to Phoenix: winner faces the top-seeded OKC Thunder; loser goes home (10 ET, Prime Video).

topStories.headline: The bracket is set — McDavid leads a stacked field as Stanley Cup hockey begins Saturday
body: The NHL regular season closed Thursday and the full 16-team playoff bracket is official. In the East: Carolina vs. Ottawa, Buffalo vs. Boston, Pittsburgh vs. Philadelphia (the Battle of Pennsylvania), and Tampa Bay vs. Montreal. In the West: Colorado vs. LA Kings, Vegas vs. Utah, Dallas vs. Minnesota, and Edmonton vs. Anaheim. Connor McDavid won the Art Ross Trophy and enters the playoffs as the league's most dangerous player. Anze Kopitar played his final regular-season game Thursday. Puck drops on the first round across multiple series starting Saturday, April 18.

stories.headline: LaMelo's Hornets enter Orlando — Curry's Warriors arrive in Phoenix with everything on the line
body: The Charlotte Hornets ride into Orlando with confidence after LaMelo Ball's go-ahead layup and Miles Bridges' game-sealing block sent Miami home. The Magic, licking their wounds after a rough night against Philadelphia, must win at home or their season ends. The locker room reportedly has internal tension. Jonathan Isaac (knee) is questionable. Winner earns the East 8-seed vs. Detroit (7:30 ET, ESPN).

In Phoenix, Stephen Curry's Warriors arrive on short rest but with enormous momentum. The Suns had a costly play-in loss to Portland earlier this week and desperately need a home win. Kristaps Porzingis is day-to-day with an ankle issue for Golden State. Winner faces the top-seeded OKC Thunder in Round 1 (10 ET, Prime Video).

stories.headline: Sixers head to Boston — Trail Blazers await their first-round opponent from tonight
body: Joel Embiid is expected healthy for Philadelphia's first-round matchup against the second-seeded Boston Celtics beginning Sunday. The Sixers have the playoff pedigree but Embiid's health all season has been a question mark. In the West, the Trail Blazers — who shocked Phoenix in the play-in — await tonight's Warriors-Suns winner as their Round 1 opponent, seeded 7th out West.

stories.headline: Carolina and Colorado headline a loaded bracket — McDavid's Oilers are the dark horse everyone fears
body: The Carolina Hurricanes are the East's top seed and enter on form, but this playoff field has no weak links. The Battle of Pennsylvania — Pittsburgh vs. Philadelphia — is already a must-watch, and Tampa Bay vs. Montreal revives a storied rivalry. Buffalo vs. Boston in the Atlantic bracket is a grudge match with deep roots.

In the West, Colorado and Vegas are the chalk, but the Edmonton Oilers — with Art Ross winner Connor McDavid — face a soft draw in Anaheim and could make a deep run. Dallas vs. Minnesota in a Central Division battle is the series insiders are circling. Games 1 across the bracket begin Saturday April 18.

stories.headline: Trout and the Angels keep slugging — the Yankees' rotation is suddenly a concern
body: The Los Angeles Angels dismantled the Yankees 11–4 on Thursday, with Mike Trout launching his fifth home run of the series. New York's pitching staff has been tagged for 18 runs in the two-game stretch — a troubling sign for a team that entered the week 8–2. Meanwhile, the Dodgers (12–3) remain the NL's class act with Shohei Ohtani's on-base streak still alive and a rotation that has given up almost nothing through the season's first three weeks.

stories.headline: PSG vs. Bayern, Arsenal vs. Atlético — the final four is a who's who of European royalty
body: PSG — who dismantled Liverpool 4–0 on aggregate — host Bayern Munich on April 28. It's a battle of the tournament's two most clinical sides: Ousmane Dembélé's devastating pace against Harry Kane's relentless goalscoring. Kylian Mbappé's Madrid has already been eliminated, so this semi-final is the de facto final for many fans.

Atlético Madrid vs. Arsenal on April 29 is a coaching masterclass in the making — Diego Simeone's defensive brilliance against Mikel Arteta's organized pressing game. Ademola Lookman is Atlético's danger man; Kai Havertz has been Arsenal's UCL clutch performer. Return legs are May 5–6. Final: Puskás Arena, Budapest, May 30.

stories.headline: Antonelli leads by 9 — Mercedes' intra-team war heads to Miami
body: Kimi Antonelli holds a 9-point lead over teammate George Russell after three races, making this one of the sport's most compelling intra-team title fights in years. Charles Leclerc (49 pts) keeps Ferrari in the picture, while Lewis Hamilton's 41 points show the 7-time champion is far from done. Lando Norris (25 pts) and McLaren are lurking. The Miami Grand Prix (May 1–3) brings the championship back to the Americas — a circuit where strategy and tire management typically shuffle the running order.

stories.headline: Zverev survives in Munich, Stuttgart's elite draw takes shape — the road to Roland Garros is open
body: The European clay season hits full stride this week with four simultaneous events. Alexander Zverev survived a three-set battle (6–3, 3–6, 7–6) against Kecmanovic in Munich, while Ben Shelton, João Fonseca, and Flavio Cobolli all advanced. In Stuttgart, the WTA Porsche Grand Prix features an elite draw: Aryna Sabalenka, Iga Swiatek, Coco Gauff, and Elena Rybakina are all in action Friday. Coming off his Monte Carlo crown (def. Alcaraz 7–6, 6–3), Jannik Sinner is the runaway clay-court favorite heading to Roland Garros on May 25.

stories.headline: Åberg's bogey-free 63 sets the pace at Harbour Town — Hovland and Im right behind
body: Ludvig Åberg opened with a flawless 8-under 63 at the RBC Heritage — not a bogey in sight around the tight, tree-lined Harbour Town Golf Links — to lead the field after Round 1. Sungjae Im eagled the par-5 second and sits in a tie for second, with Viktor Hovland showing signs of a return to form after a rough first quarter of the season. Scottie Scheffler — Masters runner-up last week — is in the field and looking to channel that Sunday 67 into a full-week performance. Round 2 is underway Friday with the cut expected around –5. Sunday decides the winner.

stories.headline: The Cup Series rolls to Kansas — Reddick leads the standings, Gibbs riding a new wave of confidence
body: One week after Ty Gibbs snapped his 131-race winless drought with that 0.055-second photo finish at Bristol, the field moves to Kansas Speedway for the AdventHealth 400 on Sunday. Tyler Reddick leads the Cup standings and is the model of consistency this season — always near the front at 1.5-mile ovals. Kyle Larson is the defending Kansas winner and will be hard to beat on a track that suits his raw horsepower. Gibbs, newly unburdened, arrives with confidence that could make him a factor too.
